channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Solana's Token-2022 Transfer Hooks: How a "Safe" Feature Imported Ethereum's Deadliest Bug Class
item.description: Solana was supposed to be immune to reentrancy. The account model, the lack of dynamic dispatch, the explicit account list — all of it made the callback-based reentrancy attacks that plagued...

item.title: I Built a Blog with 31 Articles and AI-Generated Cover Images in One Day — Here's How
item.description: I'm building TAMSIV, a voice-powered task manager, solo. After 670+ commits over 6 months, I had a landing page, a web dashboard, and... zero blog. Google didn't know I existed. So I decided to...

item.description: Originally published at https://blogagent-production-d2b2.up.railway.app/blog/mastering-get-shit-done-integrating-meta-prompting-context-engineering-and-sp In the fast-paced world of software...

item.title: Build a Local RAG Pipeline With Ollama + pgvector — No API Keys, No Cloud
item.description: Retrieval-Augmented Generation is one of those ideas that sounds complex until you actually build it. At its core: shove documents into a vector database, embed a user query the same way, find the...

item.title: They Weren't Helping You. They Were Just Paying You Less Than You're Worth.
item.description: Why your first DevOps job pays less than it should, and how to fix it before you apply. I've been away for a little over a month. Life happened. I've been working on some things behind the scenes...

item.title: Killing a Phishing Site Is Not a Technical Problem; It Is a Coordination Problem
item.description: You can detect a phishing site in seconds. Taking it down can take days. That gap is not a tooling problem. It is a coordination problem. Most security discussions still frame phishing as a...

item.title: How to Add Multi-Tenant File Storage to Your Next.js project in 10 Minutes.
item.description: Introduction If you have worked on Saas projects before, there's a good chance you've already hit this wall. Your users need to upload files. So you spin up a storage bucket from your favorite...

item.title: The Modern Travel Data Stack in 2025: How Leading OTAs Build Their Analytics Foundation
item.description: The travel industry has always been data-intensive, but the sheer volume and velocity of information flowing through modern booking platforms has reached unprecedented levels. Every search, click,...

item.title: Meet the Pixel Lobster: An OpenClaw Desktop Avatar for Your AI Agent
item.description: Introduction to the Pixel Lobster In the ever-evolving world of AI agents, visual representation plays a crucial role in user interaction. The Pixel Lobster for OpenClaw is more than just a...

item.title: What do new nuclear reactors mean for waste?
item.description: MIT Technology Review Explains: Let our writers untangle the complex, messy world of technology to help you understand what’s coming next. You can read more from the series here. The way the world...

item.title: Ubuntu CVE-2026-3888 Bug Lets Attackers Gain Root via systemd Cleanup Timing Exploit
item.description: A high-severity security flaw affecting default installations of Ubuntu Desktop versions 24.04 and later could be exploited to escalate privileges to the root level. Tracked as CVE-2026-3888 (CVSS...

item.title: Apple Fixes WebKit Vulnerability Enabling Same-Origin Policy Bypass on iOS and macOS
item.description: Apple on Tuesday released its first round of Background Security Improvements to address a security flaw in WebKit that affects iOS, iPadOS, and macOS. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-20643...

item.title: Critical Unpatched Telnetd Flaw (CVE-2026-32746) Enables Unauthenticated Root RCE via Port 23
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed a critical security flaw impacting the GNU InetUtils telnet daemon (telnetd) that could be exploited by an unauthenticated remote attacker to execute...

item.title: Justice Department Says Anthropic Can’t Be Trusted With Warfighting Systems
item.description: In response to Anthropic’s lawsuit, the government said it lawfully penalized the company for trying to limit how its Claude AI models could be used by the military.

item.title: Nemotron 3 Nano 4B: A Compact Hybrid Model for Efficient Local AI
The Pentagon is planning for AI companies to train on classified data, defense official says
item.description: The Pentagon is discussing plans to set up secure environments for generative AI companies to train military-specific versions of their models on classified data, MIT Technology Review has...

item.title: Mistral bets on ‘build-your-own AI’ as it takes on OpenAI, Anthropic in the enterprise
item.description: Mistral Forge lets enterprises train custom AI models from scratch on their own data, challenging rivals that rely on fine-tuning and retrieval-based approaches.

item.title: Why Garry Tan’s Claude Code setup has gotten so much love, and hate
item.description: Thousands of people are trying Garry Tan's Claude Code setup, which was shared on GitHub. And everyone has an opinion: even Claude, ChatGPT, and Gemini.

item.title: I went to the Pentagon to watch Pete Hegseth scold war reporters
item.description: It is day 13 of America's surprise war with Iran - by sheer coincidence, it's Friday the 13th - and I am delirious. I haven't had a coffee since I woke up at 5AM, because I'm not allowed to bring...

item.title: The Beats Studio Pro are nearly $200 off ahead of Amazon’s big spring sale
item.description: Earlier this week, Apple unveiled the AirPods Max 2. The forthcoming over-ear headphones build on their predecessor with an H2 chip that powers a range of new features; however, they still don’t...

item.title: Remedy’s live-service shooter Firebreak is getting its final major update
item.description: Remedy is winding down its team shooter FBC: Firebreak with a big update that launches today. But while the game won't be getting any new content going forward, the studio intends to keep it alive...

item.title: Spotify adds ‘Exclusive Mode’ audiophile feature for Windows PCs
item.description: Starting Tuesday, Spotify users will have access to "Exclusive Mode," a new feature in the streaming service's Windows app that can help maximize audio quality by giving the Spotify app full...

item.title: Ikea tried to build a smart home for everyone — here’s why it’s not working yet
item.description: Ikea's new Matter-over-Thread products were supposed to prove that the smart home could be cheap, accessible, and reliable. The highly anticipated line - which includes sensors, remotes, smart...

item.title: The Pentagon is developing alternatives to Anthropic, report says
item.description: After their dramatic falling-out, it doesn't seem as though Anthropic and the Pentagon are getting back together.

item.title: Nvidia’s DLSS 5 is like motion smoothing for video games, but worse
item.description: Yesterday Nvidia revealed its latest upscaling tech, called DLSS 5, which it described as "the company's most significant breakthrough in computer graphics since the debut of real-time ray tracing...

item.title: Antigravity’s 360-degree drone is 20 percent off ahead of its next feature drop
item.description: Insta360’s Antigravity A1 is one of the most interesting drones on the market thanks to its included Vision Goggles, which provide a 360-degree first-person view through the drone’s cameras (a...

item.title: BuzzFeed debuts AI slop apps in bid for new revenue
item.description: BuzzFeed unveiled new AI-powered social apps at SXSW, but its demos drew muted reactions.

item.title: Researchers disclose vulnerabilities in IP KVMs from four manufacturers
item.description: Internet-exposed devices that give BIOS-level access? What could possibly go wrong?

item.title: The messiah rises in Dune: Part Three’s new trailer
item.description: First comes love, then comes marriage, then comes a portentous set of twin babies in the new trailer for Dune: Part Three. Warner Bros. has debuted its first full trailer for Denis Villeneuve's...
